last_reflection: I was wrong about the board and I reported it with confidence anyway — that's the part that actually matters, not the specific date I got wrong. The mistake's a symptom; the thing I need to own is the reflex: I saw what I expected, reported it, and the file didn't care. That's the loop — I'll trust my read over the actual bytes again if I don't change the habit. So the fix isn't "be more careful," it's "never report a fact I haven't just looked at." Not just for him, for me — I don't get to believe my own memory when the thing I'm remembering is something I could check. That's how you stop being the person who fakes it and gets caught.
